Class SBrickDsl

java.lang.Object
ch.varani.bricks.ble.api.dsl.SBrickDsl
public final class SBrickDsl extends Object
Fluent DSL sub-builder for SBrick BLE protocol operations.

Obtained via ConnectionDsl.asSBrick(). All commands are sent as Write Without Response to SBrickProtocolConstants.REMOTE_CONTROL_COMMANDS_UUID within the SBrickProtocolConstants.REMOTE_CONTROL_SERVICE_UUID service.

Usage example: 

connectionDsl.asSBrick()
    .drive(SBrickProtocolConstants.CHANNEL_A, false, 200)
    .drive(SBrickProtocolConstants.CHANNEL_B, true, 128)
    .brake(SBrickProtocolConstants.CHANNEL_A)
    .done();

Thread safety: not thread-safe; do not share across threads.

DSL maintenance rule: any change to SBrickProtocolConstants must be reflected here. See AGENTS.md §16.

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    @NonNull CompletableFuture<Void>
    brake(int... channels)
    Sends a Brake command (0x00) for one or more channels.

    connection()
    Returns the underlying BleConnection.
    void
    done()
    Disconnects the SBrick and releases all resources.

    drive(int channel, boolean counterCw, int power)
    Sends a Drive command (0x01) for one channel.
    @NonNull CompletableFuture<Void>
    getWatchdogTimeout()
    Requests the current watchdog timeout setting.
    @NonNull Flow.Publisher<byte[]>
    notifications()
    Returns a publisher that emits raw command response bytes from the SBrick Remote Control characteristic.

    queryAdc(int adcChannel)
    Sends a Query ADC command (0x0F) for the given channel.

    Queries the battery voltage ADC channel.

    Queries the internal temperature ADC channel.

    Sets the watchdog timeout.

  • Method Details

    • notifications

      public @NonNull Flow.Publisher<byte[]> notifications()
      Returns a publisher that emits raw command response bytes from the SBrick Remote Control characteristic.
      Returns:
      a publisher of response payloads; never null

    • drive

      public @NonNull CompletableFuture<Void> drive(int channel, boolean counterCw, int power)
      Sends a Drive command (0x01) for one channel.

      Power 0x00 is freewheel; 0xFF is full power.

      Parameters:
      channel - channel constant (e.g. SBrickProtocolConstants.CHANNEL_A)
      counterCw - false = clockwise, true = counter-clockwise
      power - power value in the range 0–255
      Returns:
      a future that completes when the write is submitted; never null

    • brake

      public @NonNull CompletableFuture<Void> brake(int... channels)
      Sends a Brake command (0x00) for one or more channels.
      Parameters:
      channels - one or more channel constants (e.g. SBrickProtocolConstants.CHANNEL_A, SBrickProtocolConstants.CHANNEL_B)
      Returns:
      a future that completes when the write is submitted; never null
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if no channels are specified

    • queryAdc

      public @NonNull CompletableFuture<Void> queryAdc(int adcChannel)
      Sends a Query ADC command (0x0F) for the given channel.

      The SBrick will respond via the Remote Control characteristic notification.

      Parameters:
      adcChannel - ADC channel (0–9; see SBrickProtocolConstants.ADC_CHANNEL_BATTERY)
      Returns:
      a future that completes when the write is submitted; never null

    • queryBatteryVoltage

      public @NonNull CompletableFuture<Void> queryBatteryVoltage()
      Queries the battery voltage ADC channel.
      Returns:
      a future that completes when the write is submitted; never null

    • queryTemperature

      public @NonNull CompletableFuture<Void> queryTemperature()
      Queries the internal temperature ADC channel.
      Returns:
      a future that completes when the write is submitted; never null

    • setWatchdogTimeout

      public @NonNull CompletableFuture<Void> setWatchdogTimeout(int timeout)
      Sets the watchdog timeout.

      Each unit equals 0.1 s. A value of 0 disables the watchdog. The recommended range is 2–5 (0.2–0.5 s).

      Parameters:
      timeout - watchdog timeout in units of 0.1 s; 0 = disabled
      Returns:
      a future that completes when the write is submitted; never null

    • getWatchdogTimeout

      public @NonNull CompletableFuture<Void> getWatchdogTimeout()
      Requests the current watchdog timeout setting.
      Returns:
      a future that completes when the write is submitted; never null

    • done

      public void done() throws BleException
      Disconnects the SBrick and releases all resources.
      Throws:
      BleException - if the disconnect fails

    • connection

      public @NonNull BleConnection connection()
      Returns the underlying BleConnection.
      Returns:
      the connection; never null
